title: Book &\#x27;Half a Second&\#x27; Chronicles 2024 XZ Backdoor Incident
summary: Author Adrian Mastronardi has released a freely available book titled &\#x27;Half a Second&\#x27; that provides a detailed narrative of the 2024 XZ backdoor attempt, including the social engineering and technical aspects. This book offers a comprehensive account of a critical supply chain attack that nearly compromised millions of Linux systems, serving as an important resource for cybersecurity education and awareness. The book is released under a noncommercial, no-derivatives Creative Commons license, and is available for free at half-second.com. It highlights the lone maintainer who was manipulated, the engineer who discovered the backdoor in half a second, and the unidentified attacker.
context: In March 2024, a backdoor was discovered in XZ Utils, a compression library widely used on Linux systems. The backdoor, tracked as CVE-2024-3094, allowed remote code execution via OpenSSH and was inserted through a sophisticated social engineering campaign targeting the project&\#x27;s maintainer. The incident highlighted vulnerabilities in open source supply chain security.
